laws.title: Divorce
laws.meaning: Either party to a marriage may present a petition to the district court for dissolution of their marriage on specified grounds including cruelty, adultery, desertion, conversion, mental disorder, etc.

laws.title: Maintenance Pendente Lite
laws.meaning: Court may order either party to pay maintenance to the other during pendency of divorce proceedings.

laws.title: Order for Maintenance of Wives, Children and Parents
laws.meaning: A Magistrate may order a person to pay monthly maintenance to his wife, children (legitimate or illegitimate), and parents if they are unable to maintain themselves.
laws.punishment: Failure to pay may lead to imprisonment up to 1 month per breach

laws.title: Penalty for giving or taking Dowry
laws.meaning: Giving, taking or abetting giving or taking of dowry is prohibited and punishable.
laws.punishment: Minimum 5 years imprisonment and minimum Rs. 15,000 fine or value of dowry, whichever is higher

laws.title: Cruelty by Husband or Relatives
laws.meaning: Whoever, being the husband or relative of husband of a woman, subjects her to cruelty shall be punished.
laws.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment with fine

laws.title: Matters to be Considered by Court in appointing Guardian
laws.meaning: Court must consider welfare of the minor child as the paramount consideration when appointing guardian.

laws.act: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
laws.title: Application to Magistrate
laws.meaning: An aggrieved person may present an application to the Magistrate for one or more reliefs under the Act.
laws.punishment: Breach of protection order: up to 1 year imprisonment or Rs. 20,000 fine or both

evidence_list.name: WhatsApp/Message Records of Threats or Abuse

questions.text: What is the primary family law issue?
What is the religion of the parties?
How long have the parties been married?
Are the parties currently living together?
Has any complaint been registered with police?

recommended_actions: File complaint under Section 498A IPC and Domestic Violence Act if abuse involved
Apply for Protection Order under PWDVA 2005 for immediate safety
File maintenance petition under Section 125 CrPC at Family Court
Obtain interim custody order for children if in immediate risk
Approach One Stop Centre / Sakhi Centre for crisis support
File for divorce after mandatory mediation attempt
Document all incidents of violence with dates and photographs
